Like recently we were working with a company and they're in the analytics space.
They have like a data platform and their subject, the subject matter there is very complex, but they want the tone to be very human, conversational, like dev to dev.
And we were, we adjusted the writing guidelines to be more.
More human and conversational, but that impacted the informational quality of the content because it would condense really useful information to be more conversational.
So what our team experimented with, they added a completely separate artifact that would be applied at the draft stage.
And it would just highlight any sections where there was an opportunity for a more conversational or personable tone, and it would make those changes at that step without removing any information.
So chunking these instructions is actually more helpful for LLMs rather than just trying to have it all in one gigantic writing guideline instruction file because it doesn't always follow everything.
So that's something that we can do if we feel like, okay, like this is okay, but now it's like too conversational, or it's just like going into one extreme, we can experiment and find.

This column here to determine if a refresh is higher or lower priority or higher or medium priority, the high priority refreshes are those that saw a percentage, like a traffic decrease in the past three months and that they also, their average position is from position eight through 40.
